dividends.conditions: Lower corporate rate generally depends on direct ownership thresholds and treaty qualification. Certain pension and exempt-entity cases can differ.

interest.exceptions: Treaty analysis still depends on article-specific conditions and beneficial-ownership requirements.

royalties.type: Most royalties are generally exempt from source-country withholding under the treaty when treaty conditions are met.

articles.title: Residence
articles.summary: Defines who is treated as a resident of a contracting state for treaty purposes and supports tie-breaker analysis.
articles.fullText: This article is the starting point for treaty entitlement. In practice, it matters when a person could be treated as resident in both countries or needs treaty status to claim a reduced withholding result.

articles.title: Permanent Establishment
articles.summary: Sets the taxable-presence threshold for business profits in the other country.
articles.fullText: The PE article is central for founders, consultants, and cross-border businesses. A treaty-compliant page should explain fixed place and agent concepts rather than reducing the analysis to sales volume alone.

articles.title: Business Profits
articles.summary: Generally allocates business profits to the residence state unless the enterprise has a permanent establishment in the other state.
articles.fullText: This article is where many cross-border operating questions become real. The treaty usually protects business profits from source-country taxation unless the PE threshold is crossed.

articles.title: Dividends
articles.summary: Provides treaty limits on source-country dividend withholding in qualifying cases.
articles.fullText: The dividend article is relevant to founders and investors deciding whether treaty documentation can reduce withholding below domestic rates.

articles.title: Interest
articles.summary: Generally removes source-country withholding on qualifying interest under the treaty.
articles.fullText: The interest article is especially valuable in lender-borrower and investment situations, but the page should stress that beneficial ownership and documentation still matter.

articles.title: Royalties
articles.summary: Generally eliminates source-country withholding on qualifying royalties.
articles.fullText: This article matters for software, IP, and licensing structures. It is one of the clearest examples of a treaty changing the withholding result materially.

permanentEstablishment.dependentAgentRules: Dependent-agent analysis remains relevant where a person habitually concludes contracts or plays the principal role leading to their conclusion, subject to treaty wording and later interpretive developments.

pensions.notes: Pension treatment depends on the specific article and category of pension or similar remuneration.

sources.label: HMRC manual on convention in force

dividends.conditions: The lower corporate rate generally applies where ownership and treaty-qualification conditions are satisfied.

interest.exceptions: Specific exceptions and anti-abuse rules still require article-level review.

royalties.type: The treaty includes category-specific royalty treatment; some categories can differ from the general royalty rule.

articles.title: Residence
articles.summary: Defines treaty residence and supports dual-resident tie-breaker analysis.
articles.fullText: Residence is the gateway issue for treaty benefits. Taxpayers moving between the two countries should not jump to a withholding article before confirming treaty residence.

articles.title: Permanent Establishment
articles.summary: Determines when business presence in the other state is substantial enough to permit source-country business taxation.
articles.fullText: The PE article is a recurring decision point for founders, remote businesses, and services providers operating across the border.

articles.title: Business Profits
articles.summary: Generally allocates business profits to the residence state unless a permanent establishment exists in the other state.
articles.fullText: This article is the working core for many cross-border business questions, and it should be read together with the PE article.

articles.title: Dividends
articles.summary: Limits source-country withholding on dividends in qualifying cases.
articles.fullText: The dividend article is commonly used in portfolio and closely held corporate structures, especially where U.S. and Canadian holding companies interact.

articles.title: Royalties
articles.summary: Provides treaty treatment for royalty payments and category-specific rules.
articles.fullText: The royalty article is important for software, IP, and licensing questions. It should not be summarized as a one-number answer without checking the income category.

articles.title: Pensions and Annuities
articles.summary: Contains high-value rules for retirement distributions and cross-border pension treatment.
articles.fullText: The pension article is one of the most practically important parts of the treaty for individuals, and it is a reason Publication 597 is especially useful.

permanentEstablishment.dependentAgentRules: Dependent-agent rules remain relevant where a person acts on behalf of the enterprise with sufficient authority under the treaty framework.

pensions.notes: Retirement-income treatment depends on the article, the plan type, and the specific payment.

dividends.conditions: The reduced corporate rate generally depends on treaty qualification and direct ownership requirements.

interest.exceptions: Article-level review remains necessary for category-specific exceptions and anti-abuse conditions.

royalties.type: The treaty generally eliminates withholding on qualifying royalties.

articles.title: Residence
articles.summary: Defines treaty residence and supports dual-resident tie-breaker work.
articles.fullText: Treaty residence comes first. Users should not skip straight to dividend or royalty rates without confirming whether the person or entity qualifies as a resident for treaty purposes.

articles.title: Permanent Establishment
articles.summary: Sets the business-presence threshold that allows source-country taxation of business profits.
articles.fullText: The PE article matters for branches, project work, dependent-agent activity, and cross-border operating models that do not fit neatly into simple withholding tables.

articles.title: Business Profits
articles.summary: Allocates business profits unless a permanent establishment exists in the other state.
articles.fullText: This article is essential for founder and consulting questions and should be cross-read with the PE article and any protocol updates.

articles.title: Dividends
articles.summary: Provides treaty-based reduced withholding rates for dividends in qualifying cases.
articles.fullText: The dividend article is a core planning and documentation article for holding-company and investment structures.

articles.title: Interest
articles.summary: Generally removes withholding on qualifying interest.
articles.fullText: This article is important for financing structures and related-party lending, but documentation and beneficial-ownership analysis remain central.

articles.title: Royalties
articles.summary: Generally removes withholding on qualifying royalties.
articles.fullText: Germany-U.S. royalty structures often turn on this article, especially in software, technology, and licensing arrangements.

permanentEstablishment.dependentAgentRules: Dependent-agent rules remain important, and the later protocol/technical explanation materials should be reviewed with the treaty text.

pensions.notes: Pension treatment requires article-specific analysis and can differ by payment type.

sources.label: IRS list of tax treaties table
Germany technical explanation and protocol materials
